content.text:
<timestamp>Tuesday, Apr 28, 2026, 3:52 PM (UTC+2)</timestamp>
<user_query>
You are working on **PolitiX**, a French satirical political web application. 
Your mission covers 4 major improvements. Execute them all thoroughly and systematically.

---

## 🎨 1. COMPLETE UI/UX OVERHAUL — Satirical Propaganda Design

Redesign the entire look and feel to match a **satirical political propaganda aesthetic**:

### Visual Identity
- **Color palette**: Muted/pastel reds (#C0392B → desaturated), aged cream/beige (#F5E6C8), dirty whites, deep blacks, olive greens — like old Soviet or WW2-era posters
- **Textures**: Add CSS grain/noise overlay on backgrounds (use SVG filter or CSS `noise` pseudo-element). Every hero section, cards, and headers must feel like aged paper or screen-printed posters
- **Typography**: 
  - Headlines: Bold, condensed, uppercase — use `Anton`, `Bebas Neue`, or `Playfair Display Black` from Google Fonts
  - Body: `Special Elite` or `Courier Prime` for that typewriter/pamphlet feel
  - Use letter-spacing: wide on CTAs and section titles
- **Illustrations/Icons**: Replace generic icons with bold, hand-drawn style SVG icons (fist, megaphone, scroll, crown, sheep). Style them to look like propaganda woodcuts
- **Borders & Decorations**: Use thick borders, stamp-like elements, diagonal ribbon banners ("SATIRIQUE", "NON CENSURÉ"), distressed edges on cards
- **Buttons**: Bold, high-contrast, with slight rotation (-1deg to 1deg) for a hand-pasted poster feel. Hover effects: slight scale + ink-stamp sound feedback
- **Background**: Aged paper texture with subtle grain animation. No flat white backgrounds anywhere.

### Homepage Sections — Redesign Each One:
- **Hero**: Full-width propaganda poster layout. Big bold headline ("LA POLITIQUE, SANS FILTRE"), red diagonal accent stripe, grain overlay, CTA button styled as a rubber stamp
- **Stats Bar** (100+ PolitiXiens, 200+ Questions, 10+ Pots de vin): Display as "OFFICIAL REPORT" stamps in a horizontal band with aged paper background
- **Feature Cards** (Langue de bois, Visionnaire ou Complotiste, etc.): Style as torn newspaper clippings or propaganda pamphlet sections with bold icons and slightly skewed borders
- **Ranking/Levels section**: Design as an "official hierarchy chart" with medals, ribbons, and rank badges styled like military insignia
- **Footer**: Dark, heavy, with a red seal/stamp logo, bold copyright, and cookie notice styled as an "OFFICIAL DECREE"

---

## ⚡ 2. HOME PAGE PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION

Audit and fix all performance bottlenecks on the Home page:

### Image Optimization
- Convert all images to **WebP format** with fallback
- Add `loading="lazy"` to all below-the-fold images
- Add explicit `width` and `height` attributes to prevent CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift)
- Use `srcset` for responsive images
- Compress and optimize the hero image

### JavaScript & CSS
- Audit all JS bundles: **code-split** by route, lazy-load non-critical components
- Move all non-critical scripts to load with `defer` or `async`
- Extract and inline **critical CSS** for above-the-fold content
- Remove unused CSS (purge Tailwind/CSS if applicable)
- Replace heavy animation libraries with pure CSS animations where possible

### Data Fetching
- Implement **Static Site Generation (SSG)** or **Incremental Static Regeneration (ISR)** for the Home page if using Next.js
- Cache API responses aggressively (SWR, React Query, or built-in Next.js caching)
- Prefetch critical data during build time, not at runtime
- Replace any blocking `useEffect` data fetches with server-side props or server components

### Fonts
- Self-host Google Fonts or use `font-display: swap`
- Preload critical font files with `<link rel="preload">`

### General
- Add `<link rel="preconnect">` for all external domains
- Implement proper HTTP caching headers
- Minimize third-party scripts on initial load
- Target: **Lighthouse Performance Score > 90**

---

## 🔐 3. REMOVE VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY

Completely remove all usage of `VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY` from the codebase:

- Search the **entire codebase** for any reference to `VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY`, `VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY`, `webpush`, `web-push`, `pushSubscription`, `PushManager`, `ServiceWorkerRegistration.pushManager`
- Remove all push notification subscription logic from the frontend
- Remove all push notification sending logic from the backend/API routes
- Remove the `web-push` npm package from `package.json`
- Remove VAPID keys from all `.env`, `.env.local`, `.env.production` files and their documentation
- Remove the service worker push event listener (`push` event handler in `sw.js` or similar)
- Remove any database tables/columns storing push subscriptions if they exist
- Remove any UI elements (toggles, prompts, permission requests) related to push notifications
- Remove VAPID variables from any CI/CD config, Vercel/Netlify environment variable documentation
- Ensure the app builds and runs cleanly with zero errors after removal
- Keep the service worker file only if used for other purposes (caching); otherwise remove it entirely

---

## 🔍 4. SEO OPTIMIZATION

Implement comprehensive SEO improvements:

### Meta Tags & Head
- Add complete `<meta>` tags on every page: `title`, `description`, `keywords`, `author`, `robots`
- Implement **Open Graph** tags: `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:image`, `og:url`, `og:type`, `og:site_name` = "PolitiX"
- Implement **Twitter Card** tags: `twitter:card`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`, `twitter:image`
- Use a **propaganda-style OG image** (1200x630px) matching the visual identity
- Set canonical URLs on every page
- Add `hreflang="fr"` tag

### Structured Data
- Add **JSON-LD schema** on the homepage: `WebSite`, `Organization`, `FAQPage` (for the feature sections)
- Add `BreadcrumbList` schema on inner pages
- Add `Article` schema on question/vote pages

### Technical SEO
- Generate a **dynamic `sitemap.xml`** including all public pages and question pages
- Generate a **`robots.txt`** that allows all crawlers but blocks admin/API routes
- Ensure all pages have unique, descriptive `<title>` tags following the pattern: `[Page Name] | PolitiX — La politique sans filtre`
- Fix any broken internal links
- Ensure all images have descriptive `alt` attributes
- Implement **404 page** with proper HTTP status and links back to home

### Performance as SEO
- Ensure Core Web Vitals pass: LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1
- Ensure the site is fully **mobile responsive** (Google mobile-first indexing)

### Content SEO (Homepage)
- Wrap the main headline in `<h1>` (only one per page)
- Use proper heading hierarchy: `h1 → h2 → h3`
- Add a short, keyword-rich introductory paragraph visible to crawlers
- Ensure all CTA buttons have descriptive text (not just "Click here")

---

## 📋 EXECUTION ORDER

1. First, audit the current codebase structure and identify all files to modify
2. Remove VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY (least risk, do this first)
3. Implement SEO foundations (meta tags, sitemap, robots.txt)
4. Optimize Home page performance
5. Apply the full UI/UX redesign last (highest impact, most changes)

After each step, verify the app builds without errors. 
At the end, provide a summary of all changes made and any Lighthouse score improvements.
</user_query>

Je vais d’abord faire un audit en lecture seule pour cartographier précisément les fichiers concernés (VAPID/push, SEO, perf home, UI), puis je te proposerai un plan d’exécution structuré dans l’ordre demandé. Je commence par charger les contraintes de projet et vérifier le contexte architecture (`graphify-out*`).
Je poursuis l’audit du repo pour identifier précisément les impacts par chantier, avec deux explorations parallèles (push/VAPID d’un côté, stack home+SEO/perf/UI de l’autre).

input.prompt: Explore the repository at /home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics in read-only mode. Goal: locate every file and code path related to push notifications and VAPID keys. Search for: VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY, VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY, webpush, web-push, pushSubscription, PushManager, ServiceWorkerRegistration.pushManager, navigator.serviceWorker, Notification.requestPermission, push event listeners in service workers, backend API routes sending push. Return: 1) concise architecture of push flow, 2) exhaustive file list with why each file is involved, 3) package/dependency references, 4) env/docs/CI references, 5) potential DB schema/table references.

Explore the repository at /home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics in read-only mode. Determine framework (Next.js/Vite/etc) and identify homepage files, global styles, assets pipeline, metadata/SEO handling, sitemap/robots setup, and performance bottlenecks. Return: 1) tech stack and routing model, 2) exact files for home page UI redesign, 3) exact files for SEO (meta tags, OG/Twitter, structured data, sitemap.xml, robots.txt), 4) exact files for performance optimizations (images, lazy loading, code splitting, fonts, caching/data fetching), 5) key risks/dependencies.

content.text: J’ai maintenant la cartographie globale ; je lis les fichiers pivots (layout, home, sitemap, notifications) pour produire un plan concret avec étapes, risques, et vérifications build à chaque phase.
J’ai tout le contexte nécessaire techniquement, mais il reste 2 décisions produit qui changent fortement le plan de mise en oeuvre. Je te les pose maintenant pour verrouiller un plan exact.

input.title: Décisions clés pour le plan PolitiX

questions.prompt: Portée du redesign satirique : tu veux l’appliquer à quel périmètre ?

options.label: Uniquement la homepage publique (landing non connectée)
Homepage publique + homepage connectée (question du jour/archives)
Toute l’application (navbar, footer, pages internes principales)

questions.prompt: Quelle URL canonique faut-il utiliser pour SEO (canonical, sitemap, og:url) ?

options.label: Un autre domaine (je te le donnerai après)
Basé sur variable d’environnement uniquement (fallback local/dev)

input.name: politix-quad-overhaul
input.overview: Remove push/VAPID end-to-end, implement robust SEO foundations, optimize home performance, then apply a global satirical propaganda UI redesign across the app while keeping build stability checks after each phase.
input.plan:
# PolitiX — 4-Chantier Execution Plan

## Scope Locked
- UI redesign applies to the whole app (`global_app`), starting from homepage components and propagating to shared layout surfaces.
- Canonical/OG base URL will be environment-driven (single SEO URL builder with env fallback strategy).

## Phase 0 — Audit Baseline (read-only already done)
- Confirm framework and main entry points from [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/layout.tsx`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/layout.tsx), [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/page.tsx`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/page.tsx), [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/components/Home.tsx`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/components/Home.tsx), [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/components/HomeNotConnected.tsx`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/components/HomeNotConnected.tsx).
- Baseline perf/SEO issues to target:
  - layout is client-side and manually injects `<head>` tags in [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/layout.tsx`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/layout.tsx)
  - duplicated head management in [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/layouts/Head.tsx`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/layouts/Head.tsx)
  - robots file is misnamed (`robot.txt`) at [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/public/robot.txt`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/public/robot.txt)
  - sitemap is routed through API at [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/api/sitemap.xml/route.ts`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/api/sitemap.xml/route.ts)

## Phase 1 — Remove VAPID & Push Stack (first, lowest risk)
- Remove `web-push` runtime and typings from [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/package.json`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/package.json), then refresh lockfile.
- Delete/replace backend push logic:
  - [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/utils/NotificationSender.ts`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/utils/NotificationSender.ts)
  - [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/api/notifications/broadcast/route.ts`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/api/notifications/broadcast/route.ts)
  - [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/api/notifications/vapid-key/route.ts`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/api/notifications/vapid-key/route.ts)
  - push-send path inside [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/api/question/route.ts`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/api/question/route.ts)
- Remove frontend subscription/permission flow and related UI:
  - [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/providers/NotificationSubscriptionProvider.tsx`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/providers/NotificationSubscriptionProvider.tsx)
  - [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/components/Notifications/NotificationPermission.tsx`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/components/Notifications/NotificationPermission.tsx)
  - [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/components/Notifications/NotificationSubscriptionBell.tsx`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/components/Notifications/NotificationSubscriptionBell.tsx)
  - [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/components/IntroductionPopup.tsx`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/components/IntroductionPopup.tsx)
  - provider wiring in [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/layout.tsx`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/layout.tsx)
- Remove push listeners from workers while retaining caching SW only if still needed:
  - [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/public/notification-worker.js`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/public/notification-worker.js)
  - [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/worker/index.js`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/worker/index.js)
  - [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/public/worker-development.js`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/public/worker-development.js)
  - verify import chain in [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/public/sw.js`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/public/sw.js)
- Remove VAPID env vars from env files and docs (`.env*`, README/env docs).
- Decide data cleanup path for `NotificationSubscription` model usage: remove collection/model only if no non-push dependency remains.
- Verification gate: run `yarn build` and targeted search (`VAPID_*`, `web-push`, `pushManager`, `Notification.requestPermission`) must return zero functional references.

## Phase 2 — SEO Foundations
- Move to App Router metadata pattern with reusable SEO config utility:
  - add `metadata` in [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/layout.tsx`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/app/layout.tsx) and per-page overrides in critical routes.
  - retire conflicting manual `<Head>` usage from [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/layouts/Head.tsx`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/layouts/Head.tsx).
- Add complete meta tags/OG/Twitter/canonical/hreflang and title pattern `[Page Name] | PolitiX — La politique sans filtre`.
- Add JSON-LD:
  - `WebSite`, `Organization`, `FAQPage` on homepage.
  - `BreadcrumbList` for inner pages.
  - `Article` schema for question/vote pages.
- Replace API sitemap endpoint with native App Router sitemap (`app/sitemap.ts`) while preserving dynamic question URLs from DB.
- Replace misnamed robots file with proper `robots.txt` rules (allow all, block admin/API routes where appropriate).
- Add/verify 404 page (`app/not-found.tsx`) and internal-link integrity pass.
- Ensure image alt text coverage and heading hierarchy (`h1` unique + ordered `h2/h3`).
- Verification gate: `yarn build` + manual checks of rendered meta/JSON-LD/sitemap/robots.

## Phase 3 — Home Performance Optimization
- Reduce client-side bottlenecks on homepage routes:
  - shift blocking client fetches from [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/components/Home.tsx`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/src/components/Home.tsx) toward server/data-cache strategy (SSG/ISR-compatible in App Router).
  - keep lazy loading for non-critical blocks and remove unnecessary hydration work.
- Optimize image pipeline:
  - enforce `next/image` where possible, add explicit dimensions, lazy loading below fold, responsive `srcset` behavior, and WebP usage/fallback strategy.
- Fonts/perf:
  - replace CSS `@import` Google fonts and CDN-heavy setup with `next/font` + `display: swap` + preload of critical fonts.
- CSS/JS:
  - remove non-critical runtime scripts from head path (e.g. avoid blocking CDN injections)
  - prune unused styles and avoid duplicated global style imports.
- Caching:
  - configure fetch caching/revalidation for home data; avoid repetitive runtime calls for stable content.
- Verification gate: `yarn build` and Lighthouse run on homepage with target `Performance > 90` and CWV trend improvement.

## Phase 4 — Global Satirical Propaganda UI/UX Overhaul (highest impact, last)
- Introduce design tokens and global theme primitives for propaganda aesthetic (palette, textures, borders, stamp/ribbon motifs, distressed layers).
- Rebuild homepage sections in propaganda format (hero, official-report stats bar, torn clipping feature cards, hierarchy chart, decree-style footer).
- Extend style system to shared app shell (navbar/footer/buttons/cards/section headers) for consistent global identity.
- Replace generic icons with custom propaganda/woodcut SVG set and reusable icon components.
- Add animated grain/noise overlays and subtle rotated CTA/button treatment while preserving accessibility/contrast.
- Ensure full mobile responsiveness and semantics (one `<h1>`, descriptive CTA labels, proper section structure).
- Verification gate: `yarn build`, responsive checks, Lighthouse perf/SEO/accessibility sanity pass.

## Cross-Cutting Validation & Documentation
- After each phase: run lint/type/build checks (at minimum `yarn build`; escalate to `yarn test-all` if failures suggest regressions).
- Update docs hygiene in same work:
  - [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/README.md`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/README.md)
  - [`/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/CHANGELOG.md`](/home/lgiron/lab_perso/politics/CHANGELOG.md)
- Final report includes:
  - exact files changed by phase
  - push/VAPID removal verification results
  - Lighthouse before/after metrics summary (or explicit blockers if environment limits exact measurement).

todos.content: Remove VAPID keys, web-push dependency, push subscription/send code paths, and worker push listeners; keep only non-push notification features.
Implement App Router metadata, OG/Twitter/canonical/hreflang, JSON-LD schemas, native sitemap + robots, and robust 404/internal SEO hygiene.
Optimize homepage rendering/data fetching/images/fonts/scripts/caching to target Lighthouse performance > 90.
Apply propaganda-themed visual overhaul globally with reusable design tokens/components and redesigned homepage sections.
Run build validations after each phase and update README/CHANGELOG with all behavior and workflow changes.

content.text:
<timestamp>Tuesday, Apr 28, 2026, 4:02 PM (UTC+2)</timestamp>
<user_query>
PolitiX — 4-Chantier Execution Plan

Implement the plan as specified, it is attached for your reference. Do NOT edit the plan file itself.

To-do's from the plan have already been created. Do not create them again. Mark them as in_progress as you work, starting with the first one. Don't stop until you have completed all the to-dos.
</user_query>

Je lance l’exécution complète maintenant, en suivant strictement l’ordre du plan : je commence par marquer le premier todo en cours puis j’attaque la suppression VAPID/push de bout en bout avant un build de validation.

todos.content: Remove VAPID keys, web-push dependency, push subscription/send code paths, and worker push listeners; keep only non-push notification features.
Implement App Router metadata, OG/Twitter/canonical/hreflang, JSON-LD schemas, native sitemap + robots, and robust 404/internal SEO hygiene.
Optimize homepage rendering/data fetching/images/fonts/scripts/caching to target Lighthouse performance > 90.
Apply propaganda-themed visual overhaul globally with reusable design tokens/components and redesigned homepage sections.
Run build validations after each phase and update README/CHANGELOG with all behavior and workflow changes.

Je vais maintenant appliquer les edits de phase 1 sur les fichiers clés (providers, APIs notifications, workers, layout/settings), puis nettoyer les références restantes via recherche globale.
